title: phyllanthus retinervis
intro: Phyllanthus retinervis, also known as the net-veined leaf-flower tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and alleviating respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, studies have also shown that Phyllanthus retinervis may possess hepatoprotective properties, making it a potential natural remedy for liver health.
